items.title: AI Minister Jobs: Israel, UK Name Officials to Compete With US, China in AI Race
items.author: Marissa Newman
items.content: <article>These AI officials are at the mercy of technical breakthroughs from abroad, politics and the onslaught of AI disruption</article>

items.title: EU Escalates TikTok Probe Over Lack of Teen Privacy Protections
items.author: Gian Volpicelli
items.content: <article>The European Union escalated a probe against TikTok under its content moderation rulebook, accusing the social network of failing to protect the safety and privacy of teen users.</article>

items.title: SoftBank Weighs Deal for Robotics Startup Gravis
items.author: Min Jeong Lee and Isabel Demetz
items.content: <article>SoftBank Group Corp. is considering an acquisition of Gravis Robotics AG, people familiar with the matter said, as the Japanese investor targets the artificial intelligence technologies underpinning robotics.</article>

items.title: Bain and LY Are Said to Mull Higher Bid for Kakaku, Rivaling EQT
items.author: Dong Cao, Taro Fuse and Manuel Baigorri
items.content: <article>Bain Capital and LY Corp. are considering making a fresh joint offer for Japanese online marketplace Kakaku.com Inc., people familiar with the matter said, marking an escalation in the bidding war with private equity firm EQT AB.</article>

items.title: Vietnam Weighs Limiting Social Media Use for Children Under 16
items.author: Cao Ban
items.content: <article>Vietnam may ban children under 16 years old from posting, commenting or reacting to posts on social media as the country looks to join other governments tightening online protections for minors.</article>

items.title: Tech Stocks Face Investor Skepticism as Rally Stalls and Profit Estimates Rise
items.author: Michael Msika and Henry Ren
items.content: <article>The earnings season is confirming worries that the technology rally had gone too far, with investors now harder to please and reluctant to buy into rising profit estimates.</article>

items.title: SK Hynix, Samsung and Kioxia Face Pivotal Earnings Test on AI Swings
items.author: Rachel Yeo, Myungshin Cho and Gareth Allan
items.content: <article>SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co. are set to test investor appetite for memory chips as South Korea’s stock market — now a bellwether for global AI sentiment — grapples with violent swings driven by leveraged chip bets.</article>

items.title: Top Environmental Fund Sees Japan Key to AI Energy Challenge
items.author: Aya Wagatsuma
items.content: <article>Asia’s best-performing environmental fund has increased exposure to Japan as it expects the nation’s technology sector to prove increasingly pivotal in solving the artificial intelligence industry’s surging power demands.</article>

items.title: Robotics Startup Genesis in Talks to Raise at $3 Billion Valuation
items.author: Edward Ludlow, Natasha Mascarenhas, Yazhou Sun and Rebecca Torrence
items.content: <article>Robotics startup Genesis AI is in talks with investors to raise around $500 million in a new funding round, according to people familiar with the efforts.</article>

items.title: Nvidia Signs $1.5 Billion Accord With Amkor for Chip Packaging
items.author: Nick Turner and Ian King
items.content: <article>Nvidia Corp. signed a $1.5 billion agreement with Amkor Technology Inc. to help bolster the company’s chip packaging facilities, part of a broader push to expand semiconductor operations in the US.</article>

items.title: SK Hynix Tycoon Ordered to Pay $643 Million Divorce Settlement
items.author: Jaehyun Eom
items.content: <article>SK Hynix Inc. billionaire Chey Tae-won was ordered by a South Korean court to pay his former wife 944 billion won ($643 million) in a divorce settlement that brings the long-running feud near a conclusion.</article>

items.title: Tech Bonds Hit by Selloff as AI Debt Fears Race Through Markets
items.author: Caleb Mutua and Katherine Schwartz
items.content: <article>The bonds of some of the biggest US tech companies slid Thursday amid renewed worries about the scale of the debt-fueled artificial-intelligence boom and escalating conflict in the Middle East.</article>

items.title: SAP Cloud Growth Beats Estimates as Customer Orders Speed Up
items.author: Christina Kyriasoglou
items.content: <article>SAP SE reported better-than-expected sales for its cloud products after pushing enterprise customers to upgrade systems at a faster pace and adopt a suite of artificial intelligence products.</article>

items.title: Intel’s AI-Fueled Forecast Shows Comeback Is Gaining Steam
items.author: Ian King
items.content: <article>Intel Corp. delivered a revenue forecast that shattered Wall Street estimates as booming data center spending fuels a long-awaited turnaround.</article>

items.title: Alphabet’s Anthropic Stake Jumps to Around $124 Billion
items.author: Davey Alba
items.content: <article>The value of Alphabet Inc.’s stake in the artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC has soared to around $124 billion, making it one of the most lucrative bets in the company’s history.</article>

items.title: Trump Expands AI Data Center Pledge in Bid to Ease Power Costs
items.author: Jennifer A. Dlouhy and Jeff Mason
items.content: <article>President Donald Trump on Thursday welcomed fresh commitments from power utilities and data center developers to put technology companies on the hook to pay for the electricity they need to drive energy-hungry artificial-intelligence systems.</article>

items.title: US to Produce Ukraine’s Magura Sea Drones for the First Time
items.author: Sonya Dymova
items.content: <article>A US manufacturer is set to produce Ukraine-designed, battle-tested boat drones for the first time, as the White House bids to bolster America’s military production and expand its unmanned arsenal.</article>

items.title: Infosys Taps Veteran to Steer Software Leader Through AI Turmoil
items.author: Sankalp Phartiyal
items.content: <article>Infosys Ltd. named Ashiss Kumar Dash chief executive officer, tapping a company veteran with US experience to revive sales growth at an IT and software giant challenged by the rise of AI.</article>

items.title: SpaceX Delays Major Flight Test Again in Post-IPO Spotlight
items.author: Sana Pashankar
items.content: <article>SpaceX delayed to Friday the next major test of its massive Starship rocket after also abandoning takeoff last week, heightening the stakes for the critical milestone.</article>

items.title: Amazon Investigated Over Chinese Influence by Senate Panel
items.author: Spencer Soper
items.content: <article>Amazon.com Inc. is being investigated by the Senate Small Business Committee for allegedly letting China influence the company’s online marketplace, according to a document reviewed by Bloomberg and two people interviewed as part of the investigation.</article>
